Title: Mediation of JA signalling in glandular trichomes by the woolly/SlMYC1 regulatory module improves pest resistance in tomato

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: HD-ZIP IV transcription factor; HD, ZIP, START and SAD domains
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMS mutant identification; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; complementation by F1 cross; qRT-PCR; promoter:GUS; RNA-seq; GC-MS metabolite profiling; Y1H; biotin-DNA IP; Y2H; BiFC; LCI; pull-down; dual-LUC; MeJA treatment; spider mite bioassays
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in type VI trichomes and young tissues including meristems, young leaves and young flowers; low in mature tissues
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in type VI trichomes and young tissues including meristems, young leaves and young flowers; low in mature tissues
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Type VI glandular trichomes of leaves, stems and fruits
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chemical defence against insect herbivores; improves resistance to spider mites
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes terpene accumulation and JA-induced type VI glandular trichome formation; loss of wo reduces monoterpenes and sesquiterpenes and lowers pest resistance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential activator module component required for JA-responsive terpene biosynthesis in type VI trichomes and for proper trichome initiation/development
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Terpene biosynthesis in glandular trichomes; trichome initiation/development
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly binds TPS1, TPS5 and TPS12 promoters and, together with SlMYC1, activates their expression; function is repressed by SlJAZ2
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation via promoter binding and coactivator recruitment; protein–protein interaction-mediated JA-responsive derepression
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonic acid (MeJA); JAZ2-mediated JA signalling state
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms a JA-responsive regulatory module/complex with SlMYC1 on TPS promoters; interaction competitively disrupted by SlJAZ2
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA treatment (1 mM); spider mite feeding
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for transactivation assays; native wo promoter used for GUS reporter
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes broad trichome defects and reduced indirect/chemical defence; overexpression phenotype not established in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of young leaves (Illumina HiSeq 2500); accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to enhance glandular trichome-based defence and terpene accumulation, but editing must preserve normal trichome development because loss of function causes severe trichome defects
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A loss-of-function wo allele (W106R) and CRISPR knockouts caused severe defects in long trichomes and reduced type VI glandular trichomes. 2. wo loss strongly decreased 2-carene, β-phellandrene and α-humulene levels and reduced TPS1/TPS5/TPS12 expression in type VI glands. 3. wo directly bound TPS promoters but required SlMYC1 to strongly activate transcription. 4. wo specifically interacted with SlJAZ2, and wo was required for MeJA-induced type VI trichome formation and terpene gene induction. 5. wo deficiency reduced resistance to spider mites.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The EMS allele causes W106R substitution in the HD DNA-binding domain, abolishing promoter binding but not interaction with SlMYC1 or JAZ2; wo has broader roles than SlMYC1 in trichome development

Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; overexpression; promoter:GUS; qRT-PCR; GC-MS metabolite profiling; Y2H; BiFC; LCI; pull-down; Y1H; dual-LUC; MeJA treatment; spider mite two-choice and feeding assays
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in type VI trichomes; GUS in type VI and VII trichomes with weak expression in other tissues; expression increases during glandular cell development
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in type VI trichomes; GUS in type VI and VII trichomes with weak expression in other tissues; expression increases during glandular cell development
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Type VI glandular trichomes of leaves, stems and fruits
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chemical defence against insect herbivores; improves resistance to spider mites
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes terpene accumulation and JA-induced TPS expression; overexpression increases terpenes and pest resistance, while knockout reduces terpenes and converts type VI trichomes into small type VII-like defective trichomes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key JA-signalling modulator and activator of terpene biosynthesis in glandular trichomes; also controls glandular cell division/expansion independently of JA
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Terpene biosynthesis in glandular trichomes; glandular cell development/morphogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates TPS1, TPS5 and TPS12 expression together with wo; TPS activation is repressed by SlJAZ2
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: woolly (wo); SlJAZ2; JAZ4; JAZ6; JAZ8; JAZ10
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation through cooperation with promoter-bound wo; JA-responsive repression by JAZ2 via competitive binding
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonic acid (MeJA); JAZ2 and other JAZ repressors
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms a functional wo–SlMYC1 regulatory module on TPS promoters; module is disrupted by SlJAZ2
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA treatment (1 mM); spider mite feeding
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression and dual-LUC effectors; native SlMYC1 promoter used for GUS reporter
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression increased terpenes without increasing type VI/VII trichome number in this study; no growth penalty reported
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR-supported integration with prior JA-responsive transcriptome; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering enhanced terpene-based insect resistance because overexpression raised monoterpene/sesquiterpene levels and reduced spider mite damage, with no reported trichome density penalty
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlMYC1 is highly expressed in glandular trichomes, especially during glandular cell development. 2. CRISPR knockout reduced terpene accumulation and blocked MeJA-induced TPS1/TPS5/TPS12 expression. 3. SlMYC1 physically interacted with wo and with SlJAZ2; JAZ2 repressed SlMYC1-driven TPS activation. 4. SlMYC1 alone did not bind TPS promoters in Y1H but, together with wo, strongly activated TPS transcription. 5. Overexpression of SlMYC1 increased terpene levels and enhanced resistance to spider mites. 6. SlMYC1 additionally controls type VI glandular trichome morphology, glandular cell division and expansion in a JA-independent manner.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper concludes SlMYC1 is more important for morphogenesis of type VI trichomes than for initiation; many apparent type VII-like trichomes in knockout plants are defective type VI trichomes

Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression profiling; Y2H; BiFC; LCI; pull-down; Y3H competition assay; dual-LUC repression assay; in vitro competitive pull-down
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in type VI trichomes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in type VI trichomes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses terpene biosynthesis by inhibiting the wo–SlMYC1 regulatory module; JA relieves this repression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent repressor linking JA signalling to terpene biosynthesis in tomato trichomes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirectly suppresses TPS1, TPS5 and TPS12 activation by blocking wo–SlMYC1 interaction and repressing SlMYC1 activity
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: woolly (wo); SlMYC1; indirectly TPS1; TPS5; TPS12
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein–protein interaction-mediated transcriptional repression; competitive disruption of the wo–SlMYC1 complex
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonic acid causes JAZ2 degradation according to the model and known JA signalling logic described in the paper
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Associates separately with wo and SlMYC1 and interferes with their functional module
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in dual-LUC effector constructs
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Repression of defence terpenes would reduce pest resistance; no direct mutant phenotype tested in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for derepressing terpene-based defence, but manipulation should consider broader JA signalling consequences
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlJAZ2 is highly expressed in type VI trichomes and specifically interacts with wo among trichome-enriched JAZ proteins. 2. SlJAZ2 also physically interacts with SlMYC1. 3. JAZ2 represses SlMYC1-mediated activation of TPS promoters in dual-LUC assays. 4. Y3H, LCI and competitive pull-down assays showed JAZ2 disrupts the wo–SlMYC1 interaction. 5. The paper proposes that JA-induced degradation of JAZ2 releases the wo–SlMYC1 module to activate terpene biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although several other JAZ proteins interact with SlMYC1, only JAZ2 is supported as the specific shared repressor of both wo and SlMYC1 and therefore is the core JA-bridging regulator in this study
